programmes.show_description: False Witness: Steed investigates a blackmailer whose victims have either been killed or lost the ability to tell the truth. Can Steed trust anyone involved in the case, including Tara?
Love and Marriage: Frank blames a young Korean assistant, Mr Kwan, for his mistakes in the OR. Hawkeye and Trapper befriend Kwan and try to reunite him with his pregnant wife.
Big Mac: The camp prepares for a visit from General Douglas MacArthur, but not all personnel are excited; Klinger, in his drag, is told to keep out of sight.
Payday: Hawkeye has been given the job of acting paymaster. When the job is done, Hawkeye slips Radar a left over 10 dollars which prompts the wrath of a zealous payroll clerk.
Bad Apples: Insisting that his son finishes his education, Henry approaches his old headmaster about Matthew enrolling as a pupil at his school. But Matthew has different ideas.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
The Silent Bell: A minister faces a crisis of conscience and an exodus of ethnically mixed teachers and students when his church decrees that religion be taught in his preschool.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
The Reunion: It's Mark's high school reunion. His ageing comrades from High School must come to terms with the fact that they're not the popular young people that they used to be.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.

Heaven Nose, Mister Smith: Bob Hope guest stars as an angel dispatcher who assigns Jonathan to stop an angel intent on breaking up his son's marriage.
The Whole Nine Yards: A chauvinistic coach refuses to have girls on his high school football team.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: The Voice of Tinker Jones: Reverend Alden's request for a new church bell starts a feud between the Kennedy's and Oleson's.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: The Award: Mary is banned from taking a scholarship exam for accidentally setting fire to the barn.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: The Lord is My Shepherd: Part 1: Jealous of Charles' attention for his new son, Laura longs for her Pa's affections and refuses to pray for her baby brother to get well. Part 1 of 2.
The Great Motorcycle Race: Jim-Bob takes his first steps into the world when he enters a race, riding Ike's motorcycle.
The Pony Cart: The oldest living Walton, Aunt Martha Corrine, travels to Walton's Mountain for an extended visit.
Turf: The villagers are all at the beach for the annual horse race, usually won by Doctor Ryan. However, this year there is stiff competition from a mystery horse owned by Eamon.
A Very Ormston Christmas: It's Christmas time in Ormston, and the locals prepare for the festive period. Arthur is in a playful mood, and Deborah and Tom are struck by tragedy.
The Life of Men: Dr Finlay is faced with an inner conflict between medical ethics and legal obligations when a patient confesses to murder on his deathbed.
Rumpole and the Learned Friends: Rumpole's conduct is questioned when he accuses a police officer of framing his latest client.
Change of Command: Colonel Henry Blake has left the 4077th and Frank Burns relishes the time when he will become the new commanding officer.
It Happened One Night: Hawkeye and BJ are stuck with the overnight shift. Hot Lips tells Frank that she keeps all his love letters so he ransacks her tent to get them back.
The Late Cpt Pierce: The Army mistakenly lists Hawkeye as dead, so he takes advantage of the situation until he discovers that the dead don't get mail call and worse, they don't get paid.
To cheer themselves up Jacko and Elmo go off to a night spot for a drink and to sample the local talent.
Jacko finds himself out of a home. Eric and Jean are moving to Reigate. Veronica leaves for Sicily with Marcello, and Elmo is seen leaving the casino in his pink car with girlfriends.
Painter and decorator Jacko gets into a spot of bother after forgetfully arranging two dates on the same night. At least they're in the same pub!
Three Handed Game: The team are tasked with investigating enemy agent Juventor. Can Steed and the team stop him before he has the capability to sell secret information to the highest bidder?
Noon Doomsday: Steed is incapacitated in hospital with a broken leg when King comes to visit. Little does she know that she has led an old nemesis to Steed who is eager to exact vengeance.
Change of Command: Colonel Henry Blake has left the 4077th and Frank Burns relishes the time when he will become the new commanding officer.
It Happened One Night: Hawkeye and BJ are stuck with the overnight shift. Hot Lips tells Frank that she keeps all his love letters so he ransacks her tent to get them back.
The Late Cpt Pierce: The Army mistakenly lists Hawkeye as dead, so he takes advantage of the situation until he discovers that the dead don't get mail call and worse, they don't get paid.
All You Need is Love: Henry's objections towards Matthew's new girlfriend are intensified further when Enid makes a surprising discovery.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
The Source: A teenage girl tells school reporters that a bus driver propositioned her just before they were involved in an accident.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
The Squeaky Wheel: The difficulties of people with a disability are addressed when an amputee joins a crusade following a false fire alarm.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.

Jonathan helps Kim, a nurse at a Marine Corps post, who still suffers emotional wounds from Vietnam. Meanwhile, a teenager waiting to enter pilot training finds out he's adopted.
Hello And Farewell: Part 2: Kim agrees to get help at the veterans' counselling centre group, and Jonathan reassures Major Hastings about his son.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: The Lord is My Shepherd: Part 2: Laura thinks she caused her baby brother's death and that her parents want a son instead. She asks God to return the baby and take her instead. Part 2 of 2.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: Christmas at Plum Creek: Christmas is coming, and everyone is being secretive. Laura wants to buy something expensive for her mother but must come up with a way to pay for it.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: Family Quarrel: Divorce looms when Nels storms away from Harriet after the Olesons quarrel over eggs. The situation gets more muddled when the townsfolk try to help them reconcile.
The Best Christmas: With the Walton children growing up, will this be the last time the entire family will be together at Christmas?
The Last Mustang: Grandpa is in a determined mood when a wild mustang is captured. Will he get his wish and see the mustang roaming free on Walton's Mountain?
It's a Family Affair: It's the grand opening of Quigley's golf course, and with assistants like Liam and Donal, the event is sure to go with a bang.
A Little Touch of Harry: Arthur attempts to protect Phyllis from the charms of her errant husband when he returns to Ormston. But has Harry changed his ways?
Still Waters: An outbreak of lead poisoning at a local convent brings Drs Finlay and Cameron into sharp conflict with the Mother Superior.
Rumpole and the Heavy Brigade: Rumpole returns to the courtroom to defend a petty crook accused of murder.
Hey Doc: Sgt Kimble has done his 30 years in the army and is ready to go home to open a Korean restaurant. There's only one problem, he has too much to take with him on the plane.
The Bus: Radar is driving the bus ambulance with BJ and Frank when it breaks down. No one knows which way to turn for help so they amuse themselves by recollecting their first romances.
Dear Mildred: Potter writes a letter to his wife for their anniversary. Meanwhile, Frank and Margaret try to find the perfect anniversary gift, only to be outdone by Radar.
Likely Lads?: No Hiding Place: Bob and Terry are desperate not to know the result of the England v Bulgaria football match, but with eight hours to go, will they succeed in their mission?
...Likely Lads?: Guess Who's Coming to Dinner?: Bob thinks Terry needs new friends, but inviting him to Alan and Brenda's trendy dinner party might not be such a great idea.
...the Likely Lads?: Storm in a Tea Chest: Terry helps Bob move his most treasured possessions from his old house to the new one, but Thelma refuses to have his junk in their home.
Dead Men Are Dangerous: Can Steed and the team discover the identity of the person who seemingly holds a grudge against them before it is too late?
